basic_info.title: Final Fantasy X
basic_info.review: Final Fantasy X is a landmark JRPG that revolutionized the genre with its fully 3D environments, voice acting, and emotionally compelling story. Its Sphere Grid system, memorable characters, and iconic soundtrack by Nobuo Uematsu made it one of the most influential RPGs ever created.

alternative_names: ファイナルファンタジーX (Final Fantasy X)

play_today.details: The HD Remaster on PC with ProjectUnX mod provides the most complete experience. Includes all International version content, widescreen support, controller button prompts, and quality-of-life improvements.

recommended_patches.name: ProjectUnX (Untitled Project X)
recommended_patches.description: Comprehensive mod including 4GB memory patch, widescreen support, controller button prompts, speed boosters, and various technical fixes. Essential for optimal PC experience.

recommended_patches.name: Special K Mod
recommended_patches.description: Additional tweaks and enhancements that work alongside ProjectUnX for improved performance and visual quality.

recommended_patches.name: 4GB Memory Patch
recommended_patches.description: Fixes random crashes during cutscenes by allowing the game to use more system memory. Included in ProjectUnX but available separately if needed.

play_today.details: The original International version on PS2 offers the authentic experience with better character expressions and models than the HD Remaster. Can be played via emulator with widescreen patches.

recommended_patches.name: Widescreen Patch
recommended_patches.description: Enables 16:9 aspect ratio support for PS2 version. Available through PCSX2 patches or community mods.

optimal_settings: Use GSdx plugin with hardware mode
Enable EE x2, VU x4 speed hacks for performance

play_today.details: Console versions of the HD Remaster with cross-save functionality. Good for playing on TV or portable without PC setup.
Portable HD Remaster version with touch controls. Convenient for on-the-go play but lacks PC modding capabilities.

description.synopsis: Yuna, a young summoner, embarks on a pilgrimage with her guardians to defeat Sin, a monstrous creature terrorizing the world of Spira. Along the way she meets Tidus, a blitzball player from another dimension who has been thrust into this strange new world.
key_features: Revolutionary fully 3D environments and character models
First Final Fantasy with full voice acting in all cutscenes
Innovative Sphere Grid character progression system
Emotionally compelling story dealing with death, religion, and sacrifice
Iconic soundtrack composed by Nobuo Uematsu and Masashi Hamauzu
description.long_description: Final Fantasy X, released in 2001 for PlayStation 2, marked a major turning point for the Final Fantasy series. It was the first entry to feature fully 3D environments and character models, abandoning the pre-rendered backgrounds of previous entries. The game introduced the Sphere Grid system, a flexible character progression mechanic that allowed players to customize their party's abilities in unprecedented ways. Directed by Hironobu Sakaguchi and developed by Square's 'Dream Team' (including creators of Final Fantasy VII), FFX features one of gaming's most beloved stories about Yuna's pilgrimage to defeat Sin, accompanied by the charismatic Tidus and a memorable cast of guardians. The game's soundtrack, composed by Nobuo Uematsu with contributions from Masashi Hamauzu, is widely regarded as one of the best in the series. The HD Remaster (2016) added widescreen support, quality-of-life improvements, and included content from the International version such as additional dungeons and characters like Seymour. Despite some criticism regarding character expressions in the remaster compared to PS2 originals, it remains the most accessible way to experience this landmark RPG today.
legacy_and_impact: First Final Fantasy with full 3D environments and voice acting
Introduced the Sphere Grid progression system that influenced many later RPGs
One of the best-selling PlayStation 2 games of all time (over 8.5 million copies)
Established Yuna as one of gaming's most iconic female protagonists
The soundtrack remains highly influential in JRPG music
Set new standards for cinematic storytelling in RPGs
